title: Egghead Falls Under Siege
description: On pages 11–13, this part focuses on Blackbeard, Unknown person #10, Nami, Unknown person #11, Bonney, Lilith as CP0 attacks Egghead, Seraphim enter the conflict, Stussy betrays CP0, Kuma moves toward the Red Line, and Vegapunk’s escape plan collapses.
scene: CP0 attacks Egghead, Seraphim enter the conflict, Stussy betrays CP0, Kuma moves toward the Red Line, and Vegapunk’s escape plan collapses.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This place isn't allied with the world government!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Whitebeard's treasure has gotta be here.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you don't tell us we'll take it as rebelling against the Marines.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If we shoot each of you one at a time.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: N-NO WE WON'T! Don't point that gun at my younger brother.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We gotta give you guys money just to be treated like human beings?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Acting so tough even though Marco isn't here?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: WAAH!!!! Don't lay your hands on my hometown!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A naval ship should've been headed toward them after the warlords were disbanded

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: we couldn't come into contact with their military unit!! they got defeated.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Just as we thought, his strength is monstrous!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: get back to the ships! we'll wait for more reinforce-ment.

dialogue.speaker: Lilith
dialogue.text: the former warlord weevil has shown up!!

dialogue.speaker: Sanjuan Wolf
dialogue.text: If that huge guy hadn't come,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He really was a hero... I'm glad you're all safe.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Who do you think showed up right after that?

summary: This part advances the Egghead Under Siege through 40 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
